sourceMovie.title: Only the Brave
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Only the Brave (2017) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Saving Private Ryan
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Only the Brave clicked for you, Saving Private Ryan (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Only the Brave?
faq.answer: Try Saving Private Ryan, Oppenheimer, Richard Jewell—each shares DNA with Only the Brave while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Only the Brave?
faq.answer: Only the Brave (2017) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Only the Brave worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
